directive.title: Dashboard performance budget assertion and instrumentation

auditor.summary: The build implements the core performance instrumentation requirements well - server timing headers, client navigation monitoring, health check endpoint, and fire-and-forget audit logging. However, there is a CRITICAL TypeScript compilation error due to a missing import in lib/perf/audit-timing.ts (auditLogs table not imported). Additionally, the health check endpoint returns an 'error' status not in the specified schema. The test suite is comprehensive but has some fragility concerns. With the critical import fix and schema compliance adjustment, this build would pass all acceptance criteria.

top_issues.description: Missing import for auditLogs table. The file imports { db } from '@/lib/db' but uses db.insert(auditLogs) without importing auditLogs from '@/lib/db/schema'. This will cause a TypeScript compilation error.

Returns status 'error' which is not in the specified schema. The acceptance criteria specifies only 'ok' | 'degraded'. The error case should still return 'degraded' with a negative db_ms or handle differently.

Uses setImmediate which is not available in all environments (e.g., browser, some Node.js versions). The fallback to setTimeout is good but the type check 'typeof setImmediate !== "undefined"' may not work in all bundlers.
